entries.title: New CrystalRAT malware adds RAT, stealer and prankware features
entries.summary: A new malware-as-a-service called CrystalRAT is being promoted on Telegram, offering remote access, data theft, keylogging, and clipboard hijacking capabilities. [...]

entries.title: Apple expands iOS 18 updates to more iPhones to block DarkSword attacks
entries.summary: Apple has now made it possible for more iPhones still running iOS 18 to receive security updates that protect against the actively exploited DarkSword exploit kit. [...]

entries.title: Hackers exploit TrueConf zero-day to push malicious software updates
entries.summary: Hackers have targeted TrueConf conference servers in attacks that exploit a zero-day vulnerability, allowing them to execute arbitrary files on all connected endpoints. [...]

entries.title: New EvilTokens service fuels Microsoft device code phishing attacks
entries.summary: A new malicious kit called EvilTokens integrates device code phishing capabilities, allowing attackers to hijack Microsoft accounts and provide advanced features for business email compromise attacks. [...]

entries.title: 'NoVoice' Android malware on Google Play infected 2.3 million devices
entries.summary: A new Android malware named NoVoice was found on Google Play, hidden in more than 50 apps that were downloaded at least 2.3 million times. [...]

entries.title: Routine Access Is Powering Modern Intrusions, a New Threat Report Finds
entries.summary: Modern intrusions increasingly start with valid credentials and routine access, not exploits. Blackpoint Cyber's upcoming threat report shows how VPN abuse, RMM tools, and social engineering drive most incidents. [...]

entries.title: FBI warns against using Chinese mobile apps due to privacy risks
entries.summary: The U.S. Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) warned Americans against using foreign-developed mobile applications, particularly those created by Chinese developers. [...]

entries.title: Google fixes fourth Chrome zero-day exploited in attacks in 2026
entries.summary: Google has fixed the fourth Chrome vulnerability exploited in zero-day attacks since the start of the year. [...]

entries.title: Google Drive ransomware detection now on by default for paying users
entries.summary: Google announced that the AI-powered Google Drive ransomware detection feature has reached general availability and is now enabled by default for all paying users. [...]

entries.title: New Windows 11 emergency update fixes preview update install issues
entries.summary: Microsoft released an emergency update to fix the March 2026 KB5079391 non-security preview update, which was pulled over the weekend due to installation issues. [...]

entries.title: Claude Code source code accidentally leaked in NPM package
entries.summary: Anthropic says it accidentally leaked the source code for Claude Code, which is closed source, but the company says no customer data or credentials were exposed. [...]

entries.title: Google now allows you to change your @gmail.com address
entries.summary: Google is rolling out a new feature in the U.S. that allows users to change their @gmail address or create a new alias. [...]

entries.title: Proton launches new "Meet" privacy-focused conferencing platform
entries.summary: Proton has announced a new video conferencing service named Meet and positioned it as a privacy-focused alternative to mainstream services like Google Meet, Zoom, and Microsoft Teams. [...]

entries.title: GIGABYTE Control Center vulnerable to arbitrary file write flaw
entries.summary: The GIGABYTE Control Center is vulnerable to an arbitrary file-write flaw that could allow a remote, unauthenticated attacker to access files on vulnerable hosts. [...]

entries.title: Claude AI finds Vim, Emacs RCE bugs that trigger on file open
entries.summary: Vulnerabilities in the Vim and GNU Emacs text editors, discovered using simple prompts with the Claude assistant, allow remote code execution simply by opening a file. [...]
